_meta.description: Red-flag rules applied to scanned document text. Each rule has a weight and a plain-language explanation shown to the user. Weights sum-normalised at scoring time.

rules.label: No council file reference
rules.explanation: Genuine allocations are traceable to a specific council file or minute reference. None was found.

rules.label: No Surveyor-General / Diagram number
rules.explanation: A real stand has a survey Diagram or General Plan number. Without it, the seller's claim to the land cannot be traced.

rules.label: 'Title deeds later / once regularised' language
rules.explanation: Promising title deeds 'later' or 'once regularised' is a common stalling tactic used to take money before any real legal process exists.

rules.label: Cash-only / informal payment
rules.explanation: Genuine council transactions issue numbered, auditable receipts to an institutional account. Cash-only, non-refundable demands are a warning sign.

rules.label: Payment to an individual, not an institution
rules.explanation: Money routed to an individual (a treasurer or chairperson) rather than a council or registered account is difficult to trace and recover.

rules.label: No cooperative registration number
rules.explanation: Legitimate cooperatives are registered and can be checked with the Registrar of Cooperative Societies. No registration number was found.

rules.label: Seller is a cooperative/trust, not a title-holder
rules.explanation: Cooperatives are not always fraudulent, but a cooperative selling without council authority or registrable title is the most common source of land fraud. Verify its authority independently.

verdict_bands.label: Lower risk
verdict_bands.advice: Fewer red flags found, but still verify independently before paying.

verdict_bands.label: Caution
verdict_bands.advice: Several warning signs. Do not pay until you have verified with the authorities below.

verdict_bands.label: High risk
verdict_bands.advice: Strong warning signs consistent with known fraud patterns. Do not pay. Verify with the authorities below first.

next_steps: Deeds Registry, Harare — confirm who legally holds title to the stand.
Surveyor-General's office — confirm the stand and its Diagram / General Plan exist.
Registrar of Cooperative Societies — confirm the cooperative is registered.
City of Harare Housing Department (Rowan Martin Building) — confirm the allocation and that no demolition order is pending.
Engage your OWN conveyancer to conduct a deeds search — not the seller's.
